title: Character Set Selection Mnemonics
text: Character Set Selection Mnemonics The character set selection mnemonics instruct Uniface how to select and manipulate meta character sets. Arguments Table 1. Mnemonics for Character Set Selection Mnemonic Explanation reset Shift out of the current character set. select Shift into the character set being defined. Description These mnemonics initialize and reset the current character set, respectively. When shifting to a new character set, Uniface uses reset for the current set before using select for the next one. If the character set does not need to be explicitly reset , do not include the reset mnemonic. The following example shows the definitions used for the select mnemonic for each of the character sets defined for the standard VT display: ^BEGIN_SET 0^027(B ^SELECT... ^BEGIN_SET 1 ^027(< ^SELECT...^BEGIN_SET 2^027(0 ^SELECT Copy The VT device does not need to reset a character set before shifting into another one. As such, the reset mnemonic is not defined. This is not true of the VT340G display. The following examples show the definitions for the select and reset mnemonics used by the VT340G display: ^BEGIN_SET 2 ’T(A0(L"(0"))(U[%w])’ ^SELECT ’T(U[%(w-2)])’ ^RESET Copy Was this topic helpful?
